heading.en: Why Source Construction Fasteners from China?

body.en: fastener production with approximately 40% of world output. For African construction companies and distributors, sourcing from China offers: Cost Advantages: 30-50% savings compared to European or South African suppliers Product Range: Full range of construction <a href="/products" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">fasteners (<a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">bolts, <a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">nuts, screws, anchors, <a href="/products#washers" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">washers) Custom Manufacturing: Can produce to specific DIN/ANSI/ISO standards Scalability: Capacity to fulfill orders from 1 ton to 1000+ tons Established Logistics: Efficient export infrastructure to African ports TradeGo <a href="/products" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Fasteners has served African construction markets since 2008, supplying major construction projects in South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, and Ghana.

fastener ranges: Structural <a href="/products" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Fasteners: - <a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Hex bolts DIN 933/ISO 4017 (M12-M36) - High strength friction grip <a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">bolts - <a href="/products#anchor-bolts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Anchor bolts (J-bolt, L-bolt, straight rod) - Tension control <a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">bolts General Construction: - Hex <a href="/products#bolts-nuts" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">nuts DIN 934/ISO 4032 - <a href="/products#washers" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Flat washers DIN 125/ISO 7089 - Spring lock <a href="/products#washers" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">washers DIN 127 - <a href="/products#threaded-rods" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Threaded rods Specialty <a href="/products" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Fasteners: - Welded <a href="/products#washers" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">washers - Shear connector studs - Concrete anchors (wedge, sleeve, drop-in) - Post-installed anchors

fastener wholesale supplier: 1. Production Capacity: Minimum 500 MT/month for consistent supply 2. Export Experience: Verify Africa shipment history and documentation familiarity 3. Quality Certification: ISO 9001:2015 minimum; CE marking for European standards 4. Testing Capabilities: In-house tensile testing, hardness testing, coating analysis 5. Communication: English-speaking staff with responsive communication 6. Financial Stability: Established company with good credit rating TradeGo <a href="/products" class="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-800 underline underline-offset-2">Fasteners maintains 5,000+ MT monthly capacity, ISO 9001:2015 certification, and exports to 47 countries including major African markets.

Hex Bolt to Durban, South Africa FOB Shanghai: $1,100/MT Ocean Freight (20ft container 20MT): $1,500/container = $75/MT Insurance (approx 1% CIF): $12/MT Port Handling Durban: $50/MT Customs Duty (South Africa - Chapter 73): 10% VAT (15%): Applied to (CIF + Duty) Clearing Agent Fee: $30-50/MT Inland Transport to Warehouse: $40-60/MT Total Landed Cost Estimate: $1,500-1,700/MT Compare this to local South African pricing of $2,200-2,500/MT for equivalent quality.

fasteners from China: 1. Initial Contact (Day 1): Submit inquiry with specifications, quantities, destination port 2. Quote Response (Day 1-2): Receive detailed quotation with FOB/CIF pricing 3. Sample Approval (Week 1-2): Order and approve samples 4. Contract Signing (Week 2): Sign purchase agreement with payment terms 5. Deposit Payment (30%): Initiate production 6. Production (Week 3-4): 15-20 days production 7. Quality Inspection (Day 1-2): Optional third-party inspection (SGS) 8. Final Payment (70%): Balance payment before shipment 9. Shipment (Day 3-5): Book vessel and load container 10. Transit (Weeks 4-6): Ocean freight to Africa 11. Customs Clearance (Day 1-5): Destination port processing 12. Delivery (Day 5-10): Inland transport to warehouse Total Timeline: 6-10 weeks from order confirmation to warehouse delivery

q.en: What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ) for bulk fastener orders from China?

a.en: Typical MOQ ranges from 100-500 kg per size, with total order of 1-5 tons for mixed product orders. Some manufacturers accept smaller MOQ for standard sizes with price premiums. Custom specifications typically require higher MOQ.

q.en: Request EN 10204 3.1 Mill Test Certificates showing chemical composition and mechanical properties. Order samples before bulk. Use third-party inspection agencies like SGS, Bureau Veritas, or Intertek for container inspection. What is the typical lead time for fastener orders from China?

a.en: Production takes 15-30 days depending on order size and complexity. Sea freight to African ports adds 20-35 days depending on destination. Total lead time: 5-10 weeks from order confirmation to port arrival.

q.en: FOB (Free on Board) price includes the product cost and packaging. It excludes freight charges, insurance, and handling from the port. CIF price includes cost, insurance, and freight to your destination port. What payment terms are standard for China fastener orders?

a.en: Common terms: 30% deposit, 70% balance against copy of Bill of Lading (TT). For established relationships, some suppliers offer open account or letter of credit (L/C) terms. New customers typically pay 100% upfront or 30/70.
